topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment felt thick, heavy enough to choke on. I stared at the laptop screen, the unfamiliar script swimming before my eyes. It was a simple phrase, something I’d only ever heard spoken by my grandmother, and now, it was woven into the casual banter between my partner and a new online tutor. A knot tightened in my chest, a cold, hard pressure that made my breath catch. I picked at a loose thread on the sofa cushion, my gaze fixed on the pattern, refusing to look at him. When he finally turned, a small, almost apologetic smile touched his lips. "Hey," he murmured, his voice light, entirely oblivious to the seismic shift happening inside me. I just nodded once, a jerky movement, and retreated into the shadows of the room, the weight of the discovery settling deep in my bones.

[story 2]

I kept my hands shoved deep into my pockets, my posture deliberately slumped as I watched him speak. Every word he uttered in that melodic, unfamiliar tongue felt like a tiny, sharp pebble dropped into still water. My jaw was tight, a muscle twitching near my ear. I tried to focus on the muted television, but the sound of his voice—rich, nuanced, entirely foreign—kept pulling my attention back. *Why now?* The question echoed hollowly in my head. When he paused, waiting for a response, I just shifted my weight from one foot to the other, a restless, agitated movement. I didn't speak. I just stared at the floor tiles, wishing the concrete would swallow me whole.

[story 3]

The air in the kitchen seemed too bright, too loud. I watched him practice the pronunciation, his tongue working with an intensity that was mesmerizing and deeply unsettling. My shoulders were hunched high around my ears, a physical shield against the intrusion. I took a slow sip of my water, letting the coolness of the glass anchor me momentarily. When he finished a sentence, a complex string of sounds, I felt a profound detachment. My eyes drifted towards the window, focusing on a distant, indifferent tree. I didn't offer commentary, didn't offer encouragement. I simply stood there, radiating a quiet, profound withdrawal, letting the tension in my frame speak volumes about the sudden, unwelcome awareness of something new and deeply personal.
